findings.title: Log tail output is fully buffered in memory
findings.impact: The command accepts any positive -n value and then runs tail with capture_output=True, so a large log tail is accumulated in the Python process before anything is printed. An operator asking for a very large tail can cause high memory use or make the diagnostic command stall while buffering output.
findings.fix: Stream tail output directly to stdout/stderr instead of capturing it, or enforce a reasonable maximum line count before invoking tail.
